description: A container for a single key metric or KPI.
use_when: Displaying a single aggregate value like COUNT, SUM, AVG, or a single row with 1-2 columns.

expects.title: string (required) - The label of the metric
expects.value: string/number (required) - The main value to display

description: A responsive grid of KPI cards showing multiple metrics simultaneously.
use_when: Query returns a single row with 2 to 8 numeric columns, OR the user explicitly asks for a 'summary', 'overview', 'dashboard', or 'all metrics'. Do NOT use if there is only 1 numeric column (use Card instead). Each column becomes a separate KPI tile.

expects.metrics: array of {title, value, description, trend} — one object per KPI to display

description: A semicircular gauge showing a single metric against a target/max with color-coded zones (red/yellow/green).
use_when: Displaying a single value that needs to be evaluated against a target or maximum. Use for goal completion, budget utilization, SLA compliance. Best when the question mentions 'target', 'goal', 'how close', 'vs target', 'completion rate'.

description: A vertical bar chart for comparing values across categories.
use_when: Comparing discrete categories (e.g., sales by product, users by country). Best when you have 1 label column + 1 or more numeric columns.

expects.categoryKey: string (required) - Column name for category labels (x-axis)
expects.dataKey: string or array (required) - Column name(s) for bar values (numeric)
expects.seriesKey: string (optional) - Column name to group data into multiple bar series

description: A line chart showing trends over a continuous dimension.
use_when: Showing trends over time or sequences. Best when x-axis is temporal (date, month, year) and you have 1+ numeric value columns.

expects.categoryKey: string (required) - Column name for x-axis (usually date/time)
expects.dataKey: string or array (required) - Column name(s) for line values
expects.seriesKey: string (optional) - Column name to group data into multiple line series

description: A filled area chart emphasizing volume and magnitude over time.
use_when: Showing cumulative totals or emphasizing the magnitude of trends. Similar to LineChart but with filled areas.

expects.dataKey: string or array (required) - Column name(s) for area values
expects.seriesKey: string (optional) - Column name to group data into multiple area series

description: A dual-axis chart combining bars and a line — ideal for comparing volume against a rate or ratio on different scales.
use_when: Data has one time or category axis and EXACTLY TWO numeric columns with different scales or units. Use when the question asks to 'overlay', 'compare X alongside Y', or shows both absolute values AND rates (e.g., revenue + growth rate, orders + average order value, headcount + satisfaction score). Do NOT use if both columns are on similar scales — use BarChart or LineChart instead.

expects.barKey: string (required) - column mapped to bars (left y-axis)
expects.lineKey: string (required) - column mapped to line (right y-axis)
expects.barLabel: string (optional) - legend label for the bars
expects.lineLabel: string (optional) - legend label for the line

description: A scatter plot for visualizing correlations and relationships between two numeric variables. Supports bubble sizing for a third variable.
use_when: Data has exactly 2 or more numeric columns with no clear time axis or ordering. Best for correlation analysis, outlier detection, and positioning charts. Use when the question asks to 'compare X vs Y', 'plot A against B', 'correlation between', 'relationship between', or 'scatter'.

expects.labelKey: string (optional) - column for point labels (category name)
expects.sizeKey: string (optional) - column for bubble radius (creates a BubbleChart)

description: A bridge/waterfall chart showing how sequential positive and negative changes contribute to a final total.
use_when: Data represents a sequence of incremental changes that sum to a total or show variance. Use for financial P&L bridges, budget variance analysis, revenue decomposition, or cost breakdowns. Columns should include a step label and a signed numeric change value (positive or negative).

expects.categoryKey: string (required) - column for step or segment labels
expects.valueKey: string (required) - column for numeric change value (can be negative for decreases)

description: A funnel chart showing ordered stages with progressively decreasing values.
use_when: Data has ordered pipeline stages and a numeric count or value at each stage. Use for sales funnels, onboarding flows, marketing conversion, or any pipeline where volume decreases at each step. Use when the question mentions 'funnel', 'pipeline', 'stages', 'conversion', 'drop-off', or 'journey'.

expects.stageKey: string (required) - column for stage or step labels (ordered)
expects.valueKey: string (required) - column for the count or value at each stage

description: A nested rectangle chart showing proportional areas for categories — best for many categories.
use_when: Showing part-to-whole relationships with MANY categories (more than 8). Use instead of PieChart when there are too many slices to read clearly. Best for department spend breakdown, revenue by SKU, user distribution across many segments, or any hierarchical proportion.

expects.valueKey: string (required) - column for numeric value (determines tile size)
expects.parentKey: string (optional) - column for parent group, enables 2-level hierarchy

description: A circular chart showing proportions of a whole.
use_when: Showing how parts relate to a whole. Best for 2-8 categories. Use when the question asks about 'distribution', 'share', or 'breakdown'. If there are more than 8 categories, prefer TreeMap instead.

expects.nameKey: string (required) - Column name for slice labels
expects.dataKey: string (required) - Column name for slice values (numeric)

description: A circular gauge for showing progress or single metrics as percentages.
use_when: Displaying goal completion, percentages, or comparing 1-5 metrics. Best when values are between 0-100.

expects.nameKey: string (required) - Column name for metric labels
expects.dataKey: string (required) - Column name for metric values

description: A data grid for detailed listings when charts are not appropriate.
use_when: ONLY use when data has many heterogeneous columns that don't form a clear pattern for charts, or when user explicitly asks for a 'list' or 'details'.

expects.columns: array of {Header, accessor} - Auto-generated from fields
expects.caption: string (optional) - Table description

description: A linear progress bar for single percentage values.
use_when: Visualizing a single percentage or completion rate (0-100).

description: A small chip for status or category display.
use_when: Highlighting a status (Active/Inactive), category, or short text value from a single-row, single-column result.

description: A 2D grid visualization using color intensity to show values across two categorical dimensions.
use_when: Displaying data with TWO categorical dimensions and one numeric value. Perfect for user×month, product×region, or any matrix-style data. Use when you have many series (>5) that would clutter a line chart.

expects.xKey: string (required) - Column name for x-axis categories (e.g., month, date)
expects.yKey: string (required) - Column name for y-axis categories (e.g., user_name, product)
expects.valueKey: string (required) - Column name for the numeric value (color intensity)
